guide.entrySubtitle: Understand the main items page and the screenshot/OCR workflow.

main.title: Items page: browse slots, inspect demand, then open a target item
main.intro: The main page helps you narrow the dataset first, then jump into a specific item when something looks worth investigating.

filter.title: Filter the dataset
filter.text: Use element, mode, level, budget and class filters to reduce the item pool to the kind of builds you care about.

slot.title: Choose a slot
slot.text: The slot tabs are the main navigation. They let you focus on one equipment category and compare what appears most often there.

detail.title: Open an item for detail
detail.text: Once you click an item, you switch to the detailed recipe and OCR workflow. That view has its own dedicated guide.

overview.title: What the main page shows
overview.text: The stat cards summarize the current slice, the context strip explains what you are looking at, and the slot tabs show where item variety is concentrated.

next.title: What to do next
next.text: If an item looks promising, open it to inspect the recipe, save observed prices, run OCR on market screenshots, and compare real crafting costs.

recipe.title: Item detail: use clean screenshots for reliable OCR
recipe.intro: This view has two screenshot workflows: one to detect market prices, and one to extract stat lines from an observed item. For both, the cleaner and more consistent the screenshot is, the better the OCR results will be.

crop.title: Keep only the useful game area
crop.text: Avoid desktop borders, Discord, browser chrome, or extra UI around the Dofus window.

framing.title: Use the same framing every time
framing.text: Try to keep the price column and stats block in roughly the same position so OCR remains predictable.

quality.title: Prefer sharp screenshots over compressed captures
quality.text: Full-resolution screenshots work better than blurry phone photos or heavily compressed chat images.

market.title: Market screenshot
market.text: Use this when clicking OCR screenshot or Paste screenshot in the recipe section. The price list should be clearly readable and centered in the capture.

stats.title: Stats screenshot
stats.text: Use this when saving observed item stats. The item stat block should be fully visible, without cutting lines or mixing in unrelated tooltips.

mode.label: Calculation mode
mode.referenceHint: Compare max prices against your kibble budget.
mode.manualHint: Enter your real market prices and see the actual cost to level a level 100 familier.

reference.explainerPrefix: This budget is only used as a benchmark to compute a
reference.explainerSuffix: The real amount paid for one familier is shown in the

guide.title: Choose a mode, pick an item, read the final cost
guide.referenceIntro: Reference mode starts from a croquette budget to give you a maximum buy price per item and the total cost if you level a familier from 1 to 100 using only that item.
guide.manualIntro: Manual price mode starts from your real HDV prices and directly calculates how much a familier from 1 to 100 would cost with each item.

step1.referenceText: Enter the total amount you would pay to level a familier with croquettes. The page then converts that budget into a target XP price.
step1.manualText: Type the unit price you saw in HDV for the items you care about. The other rows can stay empty.

step2.title: Choose an item or an essence
step2.text: Use the tabs, search, and sorting to isolate the source you want to compare or buy in a loop.

step3.title: Read the last column first
step3.referenceText: Coût total au prix cible is the real estimated total to level a familier from 1 to 100 with that item only.
step3.manualText: Coût total lvl 100 is the real estimated total to level a familier from 1 to 100 with that item only.

columns.title: Columns
columns.qtyText: Number of units needed to level from 1 to 100.
columns.referencePriceText: recommended maximum buy price per unit.

example.title: Example
example.line1: If a row shows 13 815 in quantity and 362 k in unit price, you need about 13 815 units.
example.line2: The final total will therefore be about 13 815 × 362, which lands close to the reference budget.
